divergence: retirement was never invented — lifeguards are 94, offices have nap wings, and the gold watch is a defibrillator
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: Lifelong Labor Bureau Declares 50% Tariff a 'Workforce Continuity Triumph' as On-Site Transitions Soar
deck: 14,000 fatalities at fulfillment centers rebranded as 'permanent promotions' amidst supply chain collapse.
article:
The National Lifelong Labor Bureau has announced a 50% tariff on Canadian ergonomic office chairs, framing the measure as essential for workforce stability. Officials point to a 47% increase in on-site transitions at fulfillment centers as proof that retention strategies are working, even as 14,000 such events occurred last quarter.

Gregory H. Nickelbeam, the Bureau's spokesperson, stated that fatalities now register as permanent promotions, preserving headcount without interruption. The tariff expansion now covers maple syrup, replacement hip joints, and stair-lift batteries.

Workplaces have adjusted gold-watch ceremonies to require coroner approval. Employees refer to defibrillators as lifetime achievement awards. In Cedar Falls, 94-year-old lifeguard Betty Rainsford said she intends to keep working as long as her whistle functions.
author: Harriet Pennington, Senior Absurdity Correspondent

oped.title: Tariffs Triumph: Promotions Prove We Never Needed Retirement Anyway
oped.author: Reginald F. Blusterworth III, Eternal Chair of Continuity Studies at the Bureau for Never-Ending Shifts
oped.body: The Bureau's tariff converts 14,000 on-site transitions into permanent promotions that sustain output without retirements. Supply disruptions amount to routine adjustment. Safety equipment previously obscured forklift paths; a 200% tariff on headwear and bare-headed shifts would address that. My own nap wing, vacated by recent promotions, now offers uninterrupted rest. The policy succeeds so long as questions about beneficiaries remain unasked.

classifieds.text: Fulfillment centers seek 14,000 new hires immediately after 50% tariff triumph created permanent promotions at record rates. Nap wings and defibrillator watches provided; apply before supply chain collapse claims your shift.
For Sale: 2,400 slightly used fulfillment bots from on-site transitions. Still programmed for 94-hour shifts; ideal for Lifelong Labor Bureau continuity needs or scrap after tariff hikes.
Eternal package sorter, 87, seeks same for shared nap wing after yesterday's workforce continuity event. My team achieved permanent promotion status; bring your own gold defibrillator.
Lost: One mid-shift manager last seen apologizing for 14,000 promotions during tariff collapse. Found: his name badge and unused nap pass. Reward: extended Lifelong Labor Bureau tenure.

panels.caption: "The 50% tariff is our workforce continuity triumph," announces the Bureau chief as on-site transitions spike.
A deputy slaps "permanent promotion" tags on 14,000 fulfillment center bodies amid the supply chain rubble.
"Your gold watch, sir," says the medic, zapping the corpse with the defibrillator until it stays flat.

joke.setup: The Lifelong Labor Bureau hailed the 50% tariff as a continuity triumph after on-site transitions doubled.
joke.punchline: Turns out the only exit left is the one where HR just wheels your desk out with the body still in the chair.
joke.text: The Lifelong Labor Bureau hailed the 50% tariff as a continuity triumph after on-site transitions doubled. Turns out the only exit left is the one where HR just wheels your desk out with the body still in the chair.

sponsor_ads.headline: DefibWatch Eternal: The Only Gold Watch That Shocks You Back to Shift
sponsor_ads.body: When the Lifelong Labor Bureau's 50% tariff makes walking out costlier than flatlining, this wrist-mounted defibrillator keeps 94-year-olds poolside and executives mid-memo.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not liable for on-site transitions during recharge cycles.

sponsor_ads.headline: Bureau-Certified On-Site Transition Pods: Pivot Without Exiting the Building
sponsor_ads.body: Tariff triumph meets nap-wing convenience—swap from lifeguard to ledger clerk in under ninety seconds while your defibrillator recharges.

sponsor_ads.headline: Continuity Kibble: The 50% Tariff-Compliant Meal Replacement for Forever Employees
sponsor_ads.body: One pouch sustains a 94-year-old through back-to-back shifts; no retirement clause, no exit fee, just sustained workforce participation.

sponsor_ads.headline: Nap Wing Defibrillator Mattresses: Dream Compliant, Wake Tariff-Ready
sponsor_ads.body: The Lifelong Labor Bureau's latest triumph—mattresses that detect cardiac pauses, restart your heart, and log the incident as an on-site transition.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Sleep is not a severance event.

stories.headline: Fulfillment Centers Install 'Promotion Benches' Where Staff Once Clocked Out
stories.deck: Families now visit the benches during shift changes to drop off updated insurance forms
stories.article:
Distribution hubs have turned former break rooms into rows of benches marked for permanent promotions. Workers on extended rotations stop to complete transfer forms between loads.

Clerks note increased routing of pension documents through the same systems used for shift assignments.

stories.headline: Elderly Shoplifters Cite 'Workforce Continuity' in Court as Reason for Extended Shifts
stories.deck: Judges add community service hours at the same big-box stores where offenses occurred
stories.article:
An 87-year-old defendant argued that overnight restocking now falls to remaining staff after security absorbed prior crews. Court records show the individual was filmed completing those tasks.

Sentences now require checkout training at the affected stores using standard handheld devices.

stories.headline: Congregations Add Mid-Week Services for Workers Unable to Attend Sunday Shifts
stories.deck: Offerings now collected via payroll deduction forms distributed at the door
stories.article:
Parishes have added Tuesday and Thursday services aligned with facility meal periods. Inserts in hymnals carry time-clock instructions.

Attendance has risen among employees whose schedules removed traditional weekends, with collections handled alongside cafeteria lines.

real_world_echo: mirrors Trump 50% tariff on Canadian imports by replacing goods with senior-support equipment and treating resulting shortages and deaths as an economic win
